question.text: Which of the following is NOT a typical component of a comprehensive financial plan?

answer.text: Cash flow analysis and budget preparation
Insurance needs assessment and estate planning
Guaranteed stock market returns for the next 10 years
Tax planning and retirement corpus estimation

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A comprehensive financial plan includes cash flow analysis, budgeting, investment planning, insurance needs, estate planning, tax planning, and retirement planning. No plan can guarantee stock market returns. **The Trap:** Return projections may be used (based on assumptions), but guarantees are never part of any legitimate financial plan. All other options are standard components. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A Chapter 1 covers the full scope of financial planning. This concept is tested across all advisory-related NISM exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use historical return ranges and probability-based scenarios instead of point estimates when projecting future returns in financial plans — this sets realistic expectations.
